001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.lang.reflect.*;
021import java.nio.charset.*;
022
023import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
027
028/**
029 * Top-level class for a pairing of a {@link Serializer} and {@link Parser} into a single class with convenience read/write methods.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 *    The general idea is to combine a single serializer and parser inside a simplified API for reading and writing POJOs.
033 *
034 * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
035 * <p class='bjava'>
036 *    <jc>// Using instance.</jc>
037 *    Marshaller <jv>json</jv> = <jk>new</jk> Json();
038 *    MyPojo <jv>myPojo</jv> = <jv>json</jv>.to(<jv>string</jv>, MyPojo.<jk>class</jk>);
039 *    String <jv>string</jv> = <jv>json</jv>.of(<jv>myPojo</jv>);
040 * </p>
041 * <p class='bjava'>
042 * <jc>// Using DEFAULT instance.</jc>
043 *    MyPojo <jv>myPojo</jv> = Json.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.to(<jv>string</jv>, MyPojo.<jk>class</jk>);
044 *    String <jv>string</jv> = Json.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.of(<jv>myPojo</jv>);
045 * </p>
046 *
047 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
048 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Marshallers">Marshallers</a>
049 * </ul>
050 */
051@SuppressWarnings({
052   "resource" // Cursor shortcut methods return Closeables owned by the caller; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
053})
054public abstract class Marshaller {
055   private final Serializer s;
056   private final Parser p;
057
058   /**
059    * Constructor.
060    *
061    * @param s
062    *    The serializer to use for serializing output.
063    *    <br>Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
064    * @param p
065    *    The parser to use for parsing input.
066    *    <br>Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
067    */
068   protected Marshaller(Serializer s, Parser p) {
069      this.s = s;
070      this.p = p;
071   }
072
073   /**
074    * Returns the parser associated with this marshaller.
075    *
076    * @return The parser associated with this marshaller.
077    */
078   public Parser getParser() { return p; }
079
080   /**
081    * Returns the serializer associated with this marshaller.
082    *
083    * @return The serializer associated with this marshaller.
084    */
085   public Serializer getSerializer() { return s; }
086
087   /**
088    * Same as {@link #to(Object, Type, Type...)} except optimized for a non-parameterized class.
089    *
090    * <p>
091    * This is the preferred parse method for simple types since you don't need to cast the results.
092    *
093    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
094    * <p class='bjava'>
095    *    Marshaller <jv>marshaller</jv>  = Json.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>;
096    *
097    *    <jc>// Parse into a string.</jc>
098    *    String <jv>string</jv> = <jv>marshaller</jv> .to(<jv>json</jv>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
099    *
100    *    <jc>// Parse into a bean.</jc>
101    *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>marshaller</jv> .to(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
102    *
103    *    <jc>// Parse into a bean array.</jc>
104    *    MyBean[] <jv>beanArray</jv> = <jv>marshaller</jv> .to(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean[].<jk>class</jk>);
105    *
106    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of objects.</jc>
107    *    List <jv>list</jv> = <jv>marshaller</jv> .to(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>);
108    *
109    *    <jc>// Parse into a map of object keys/values.</jc>
110    *    Map <jv>map</jv> = <jv>marshaller</jv> .to(<jv>json</jv>, TreeMap.<jk>class</jk>);
111    * </p>
112    *
113    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
114    * @param input
115    *    The input.
116    *    <br>Character-based parsers can handle the following input class types:
117    *    <ul>
118    *       <li><jk>null</jk>
119    *       <li>{@link Reader}
120    *       <li>{@link CharSequence}
121    *       <li>{@link InputStream} containing UTF-8 encoded text (or charset defined by
122    *          {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ReaderParser.Builder#streamCharset(Charset)} property value).
123    *       <li><code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code> containing UTF-8 encoded text (or charset defined by
124    *          {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ReaderParser.Builder#streamCharset(Charset)} property value).
125    *       <li>{@link File} containing system encoded text (or charset defined by
126    *          {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ReaderParser.Builder#fileCharset(Charset)} property value).
127    *    </ul>
128    *    <br>Stream-based parsers can handle the following input class types:
129    *    <ul>
130    *       <li><jk>null</jk>
131    *       <li>{@link InputStream}
132    *       <li><code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>
133    *       <li>{@link File}
134    *       <li>{@link CharSequence} containing encoded bytes according to the {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.InputStreamParser.Builder#binaryFormat(BinaryFormat)} setting.
135    *    </ul>
136    * @param type The object type to create.
137    * @return The parsed object.
138    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
139    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
140    */
141   public final <T> T to(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
142      return p.parse(input, type);
143   }
144
145   /**
146    * Parses input into the specified object type.
147    *
148    * <p>
149    * The type can be a simple type (e.g. beans, strings, numbers) or parameterized type (collections/maps).
150    *
151    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
152    * <p class='bjava'>
153    *    Marshaller <jv>marshaller</jv> = Json.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>;
154    *
155    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of strings.</jc>
156    *    List <jv>list1</jv> = <jv>marshaller</jv> .to(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
157    *
158    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of beans.</jc>
159    *    List <jv>list2</jv> = <jv>marshaller</jv> .to(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
160    *
161    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of linked-lists of strings.</jc>
162    *    List <jv>list3</jv> = <jv>marshaller</jv> .to(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
163    *
164    *    <jc>// Parse into a map of string keys/values.</jc>
165    *    Map <jv>map1</jv> = <jv>marshaller</jv> .to(<jv>json</jv>, TreeMap.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
166    *
167    *    <jc>// Parse into a map containing string keys and values of lists containing beans.</jc>
168    *    Map <jv>map2</jv> = <jv>marshaller</jv> .to(<jv>json</jv>, TreeMap.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, List.<jk>class</jk>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
169    * </p>
170    *
171    * <p>
172    * <c>Collection</c> classes are assumed to be followed by zero or one objects indicating the element type.
173    *
174    * <p>
175    * <c>Map</c> classes are assumed to be followed by zero or two meta objects indicating the key and value types.
176    *
177    * <p>
178    * The array can be arbitrarily long to indicate arbitrarily complex data structures.
179    *
180    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
181    *    <li class='note'>
182    *       Use the {@link #to(Object, Class)} method instead if you don't need a parameterized map/collection.
183    * </ul>
184    *
185    * @param <T> The class type of the object to create.
186    * @param input
187    *    The input.
188    *    <br>Character-based parsers can handle the following input class types:
189    *    <ul>
190    *       <li><jk>null</jk>
191    *       <li>{@link Reader}
192    *       <li>{@link CharSequence}
193    *       <li>{@link InputStream} containing UTF-8 encoded text (or charset defined by
194    *          {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ReaderParser.Builder#streamCharset(Charset)} property value).
195    *       <li><code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code> containing UTF-8 encoded text (or charset defined by
196    *          {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ReaderParser.Builder#streamCharset(Charset)} property value).
197    *       <li>{@link File} containing system encoded text (or charset defined by
198    *          {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ReaderParser.Builder#fileCharset(Charset)} property value).
199    *    </ul>
200    *    <br>Stream-based parsers can handle the following input class types:
201    *    <ul>
202    *       <li><jk>null</jk>
203    *       <li>{@link InputStream}
204    *       <li><code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>
205    *       <li>{@link File}
206    *       <li>{@link CharSequence} containing encoded bytes according to the {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.InputStreamParser.Builder#binaryFormat(BinaryFormat)} setting.
207    *    </ul>
208    * @param type
209    *    The object type to create.
210    *    <br>Can be any of the following: {@link ClassMeta}, {@link Class}, {@link ParameterizedType}, {@link GenericArrayType}
211    * @param args
212    *    The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
213    *    <br>Can be any of the following: {@link ClassMeta}, {@link Class}, {@link ParameterizedType}, {@link GenericArrayType}
214    *    <br>Ignored if the main type is not a map or collection.
215    * @return The parsed object.
216    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
217    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
218    * @see MarshallingSession#getClassMeta(Type,Type...) for argument syntax for maps and collections.
219    */
220   public final <T> T to(Object input, Type type, Type...args) throws ParseException, IOException {
221      return p.parse(input, type, args);
222   }
223
224   /**
225    * Serializes a POJO to the specified output stream or writer.
226    *
227    * <p>
228    * Equivalent to calling <c>serializer.createSession().serialize(o, output);</c>
229    *
230    * @param object The object to serialize.
231    * @param output
232    *    The output object.
233    *    <br>Character-based serializers can handle the following output class types:
234    *    <ul>
235    *       <li>{@link Writer}
236    *       <li>{@link OutputStream} - Output will be written as UTF-8 encoded stream.
237    *       <li>{@link File} - Output will be written as system-default encoded stream.
238    *       <li>{@link StringBuilder} - Output will be written to the specified string builder.
239    *    </ul>
240    *    <br>Stream-based serializers can handle the following output class types:
241    *    <ul>
242    *       <li>{@link OutputStream}
243    *       <li>{@link File}
244    *    </ul>
245    * @throws SerializeException If a problem occurred trying to convert the output.
246    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
247    */
248   public final void of(Object object, Object output) throws SerializeException, IOException {
249      s.serialize(object, output);
250   }
251
252   /**
253    * Opens a low-level {@link TokenReader} cursor over the specified input.
254    *
255    * <p>
256    * Shortcut for <c>((TokenReadable)getParser()).parseTokens(<jv>input</jv>)</c>.  See
257    * {@link TokenReader} for the cursor contract.
258    *
259    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
260    *    <li class='note'>This capability depends on the underlying parser.  Formats whose parser
261    *       does not implement {@link TokenReadable} (the structural-token role) yield a
262    *       {@link ClassCastException}.
263    * </ul>
264    *
265    * @param input The input.  Accepts {@link Reader}, {@link CharSequence}, {@link InputStream},
266    *    <code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>, or {@link File}.
267    * @return A new {@link TokenReader} cursor.
268    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
269    */
270   public TokenReader fromTokens(Object input) throws IOException {
271      return ((TokenReadable) getParser()).parseTokens(input);
272   }
273
274   /**
275    * Opens a low-level push generator that emits structural events one at a time.
276    *
277    * <p>
278    * Shortcut for <c>((TokenWritable)getSerializer()).serializeTokens(<jv>output</jv>)</c>.  See
279    * {@link TokenWriter} for the generator contract.
280    *
281    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
282    *    <li class='note'>This capability depends on the underlying serializer.  Formats whose
283    *       serializer does not implement {@link TokenWritable} (the structural-token role) yield a
284    *       {@link ClassCastException}.
285    * </ul>
286    *
287    * @param output The output.  Accepts {@link Writer}, {@link OutputStream}, {@link File}, or
288    *    {@link StringBuilder}.
289    * @return A new {@link TokenWriter}.
290    * @throws IOException If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.
291    */
292   public TokenWriter toTokens(Object output) throws IOException {
293      return ((TokenWritable) getSerializer()).serializeTokens(output);
294   }
295
296   /**
297    * Opens a low-level {@link RecordReader} cursor that yields each top-level value as a record.
298    *
299    * <p>
300    * Shortcut for <c>((RecordReadable)getParser()).parseRecords(<jv>input</jv>)</c>.  See
301    * {@link RecordReader} for the cursor contract.
302    *
303    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
304    *    <li class='note'>This capability depends on the underlying parser.  Formats whose parser
305    *       does not implement {@link RecordReadable} yield a {@link ClassCastException}.
306    * </ul>
307    *
308    * @param input The input.  Accepts {@link Reader}, {@link CharSequence}, {@link InputStream},
309    *    <code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>, or {@link File}.
310    * @return A new {@link RecordReader} cursor.
311    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
312    */
313   public RecordReader fromRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
314      return ((RecordReadable) getParser()).parseRecords(input);
315   }
316
317   /**
318    * Opens a low-level {@link RecordWriter} cursor that emits each whole value as a record.
319    *
320    * <p>
321    * Shortcut for <c>((RecordWritable)getSerializer()).serializeRecords(<jv>output</jv>)</c>.  See
322    * {@link RecordWriter} for the cursor contract.
323    *
324    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
325    *    <li class='note'>This capability depends on the underlying serializer.  Formats whose
326    *       serializer does not implement {@link RecordWritable} yield a {@link ClassCastException}.
327    * </ul>
328    *
329    * @param output The output.  Accepts {@link Writer}, {@link OutputStream}, {@link File}, or
330    *    {@link StringBuilder}.
331    * @return A new {@link RecordWriter} cursor.
332    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.
333    */
334   public RecordWriter toRecords(Object output) throws IOException {
335      return ((RecordWritable) getSerializer()).serializeRecords(output);
336   }
337
338   /**
339    * Opens a {@link RecordReader} that yields each element of a top-level wire array as a
340    * separate record.
341    *
342    * <p>
343    * Shortcut for <c>((ArrayRecordReadable)getParser()).parseArrayRecords(<jv>input</jv>)</c>.
344    *
345    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
346    *    <li class='note'>This capability depends on the underlying parser.  Formats whose parser
347    *       does not implement {@link ArrayRecordReadable} yield a {@link ClassCastException}.
348    * </ul>
349    *
350    * @param input The input.  Accepts {@link Reader}, {@link CharSequence}, {@link InputStream},
351    *    <code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>, or {@link File}.
352    * @return A new element-streamed {@link RecordReader} cursor.
353    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
354    */
355   public RecordReader fromArrayRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
356      return ((ArrayRecordReadable) getParser()).parseArrayRecords(input);
357   }
358
359   /**
360    * Opens a {@link RecordWriter} that wraps each written value as one element of a top-level
361    * wire array.
362    *
363    * <p>
364    * Shortcut for <c>((ArrayRecordWritable)getSerializer()).serializeArrayRecords(<jv>output</jv>)</c>.
365    *
366    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
367    *    <li class='note'>This capability depends on the underlying serializer.  Formats whose
368    *       serializer does not implement {@link ArrayRecordWritable} yield a
369    *       {@link ClassCastException}.
370    * </ul>
371    *
372    * @param output The output.  Accepts {@link Writer}, {@link OutputStream}, {@link File}, or
373    *    {@link StringBuilder}.
374    * @return A new element-streamed {@link RecordWriter} cursor.
375    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.
376    */
377   public RecordWriter toArrayRecords(Object output) throws IOException {
378      return ((ArrayRecordWritable) getSerializer()).serializeArrayRecords(output);
379   }
380}
